title: esenbeckia pumila var. leucophylla
intro: Esenbeckia pumila var. leucophylla is a variety of the Esenbeckia pumila plant, commonly known as the 'white-leaved' or 'white-flowered' variety, which is a type of flowering plant in the Rutaceae family. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Brazil and other parts of South America, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and alleviating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, likely due to its flavonoid and terpene content. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Esenbeckia pumila var. leucophylla are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
